text.title: synthetic-users Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for synthetic-users as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running a single synthetic participant and treating the output as representative.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-participant synthetic outputs have no statistical meaning; run at least 5 per concept for any directional signal.

failure-mode.description: Using synthetic users for specialist domain research (medicine, law, finance) without explicit bias warnings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Synthetic users in specialist fields hallucinate plausible-sounding domain knowledge; outputs cannot be used to validate domain-specific product decisions.

failure-mode.description: Agent playing a synthetic user breaks character when probed on technical details — answers as AI, not as persona.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM simulation degrades under probing; deep technical questions reveal the AI substrate; these responses are not valid as synthetic user data.

failure-mode.description: Synthetic users display implausible product knowledge in simulation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI models are trained on product descriptions on the internet; synthetic personas know more about your product than a real user would. Prime explicitly: instruct the persona to have limited product awareness.

failure-mode.description: Agent-analyzed themes from synthetic interviews lack evidence quotes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Synthetic themes have double hallucination risk (simulation + analysis); require evidence quotes for every extracted theme to surface fabrication.
